title: Review round 1: wolfhsm crate core API
notes:
Maintainer risk: HIGH — unsound use-after-free in CryptoCbGuard/Client drop ordering, hardcoded client_id=1 defeats server ACL, and two error-type misuse patterns that will spread to future contributors; all three require API-breaking fixes to resolve cleanly
Confidence: 80% chance of no missed critical bug in the 8 files reviewed. Uncertainty driven by: (1) I cannot link and run the code — the pointer aliasing in connect() (get_unchecked_mut after Box::pin) is visually correct but not verified under Miri or under actual wolfHSM server calls. (2) The CryptoCb lifetime contract (guard must be dropped before Client) is documented in a comment but not enforced by the type system — if a caller drops Client while CryptoCbGuard is live, the C layer has a dangling context pointer. (3) The double-check pattern in nvm_available/nvm_metadata/nvm_read (check transport rc then check server out_rc) is correct per the C API contract, but that contract is only in the wolfHSM header comments, not verifiable here. Would raise confidence if: (a) Miri run on connect() and drop() passes, (b) CryptoCbGuard is given a PhantomData<&'client mut Client> borrow to enforce ordering at compile time (this would require API change), (c) integration test exercising drop ordering exists. Are we running out of real complaints? Yes, substantially. The 8 core files are clean, well-commented, and follow sound Rust FFI patterns. The remaining open questions are either (i) real but minor or require API change, or (ii) unverifiable without a running server. The review is not manufacturing problems — there are no dead code, no missing error checks, no obvious logic errors to report.
